title: 10 best herbal decoctions for chills
intro_desc: Herbal decoctions for chills are warm, comforting beverages made by steeping herbs in hot water to release their medicinal properties. These decoctions help alleviate chills by stimulating the body's natural warming mechanisms and soothing cold-related discomforts. Examples of herbal decoctions that can help with chills include ginger tea, which warms the stomach and improves circulation, and peppermint tea, which cools and relaxes the body. By sipping on these teas, people suffering from chills can find relief and improve their overall quality of life.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Purple coneflower decoctions helps with chills because of its antipyretic properties, which help to reduce fever and relieve discomfort. The decoction's bioactive compounds, including rosmarinic acid and quercetin, have anti-inflammatory effects that soothe the body and calm shivering. Additionally, the decoction's antioxidant properties help to combat oxidative stress, which can contribute to chills. By addressing these underlying causes, purple coneflower decoctions provide natural relief from chills, helping to promote a sense of warmth and comfort.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: Purple Coneflower's antioxidants help neutralize free radicals, reducing inflammation and oxidative stress that can contribute to chills.

Immunomodulatory effects: The decoction's active compounds, such as echinacein and cinnamic acid, modulate the immune system by enhancing the production of cytokines, which helps fight off infections that cause chills.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Purple Coneflower contains compounds like sesquiterpenes and flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory effects, reducing the severity of inflammatory responses associated with chills, such as fever and body aches.
remedy_constituents: Ishagerins: These sesquiterpene lactones may help with chills by exerting anti-inflammatory effects and modulating immune responses.
Cichoric acid: A phenolic compound, cichoric acid may help with chills by exhibiting antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate symptoms associated with infections.
Alkylamides: These non-nitrogenous compounds may help with chills by increasing the production of cytokines, which are proteins that help fight off infections and reduce inflammation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Echinacea purpurea roots are one of the most used parts due to their high concentration of alkaloids and glycosides, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory properties.
Flowers: Echinacea purpurea flowers are widely used for their immunomodulatory effects, helping to boost the body's natural defenses against infections and chills.
Leaves: Echinacea purpurea leaves are often utilized due to their rich content of caffeic acid and other phenolic compounds, which exhibit antimicrobial and antioxidant properties.
remedy_recipe: Gather 30 grams of dried echinacea purpurea flowers and leaves from a trusted source.
Crush the dried echinacea purpurea flowers and leaves into a fine powder using a mortar.
Combine 1 teaspoon of the powdered echinacea purpurea with 250 milliliters of boiling water in a pot.
Steep the mixture for 5 to 10 minutes to allow the active compounds to infuse.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree decoctions helps with chills because of its unique properties that help to calm the body's natural response to cold temperatures. The decoction is rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, which work together to reduce inflammation and ease congestion in the nasal passages and sinuses. Additionally, the decoction's warming effects help to increase blood flow and relax muscles, making it easier for the body to regulate its temperature and overcome chills.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties: Adiantum spp. has been shown to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which may help alleviate symptoms such as fever or chills associated with infections.

Expectorant and antitussive properties: The decoction of Adiantum spp. has been used traditionally to relieve respiratory issues like coughs and bronchitis, which could potentially help reduce symptoms of chills.

Immunomodulatory effects: Some studies suggest that Adiantum spp. may have immunomodulatory effects, enhancing the body's natural defense against infections and reducing the severity of symptoms like fever and chills.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These compounds help reduce inflammation and improve blood flow, which can help alleviate the sensation of chills and coldness associated with poor circulation.
Bilobalide: This triterpene lactone has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help mitigate the effects of cold stress and chills by protecting the body against oxidative damage.

Quercetin: This flavonoid acts as a potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory agent, helping to reduce the severity of chills and coldness by preventing the formation of pro-inflammatory molecules and promoting vasodilation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Ginkgo biloba leaves are the most commonly used part, as they contain flavonoids and terpenoids that help to improve blood circulation and reduce inflammation.
Seeds: Ginkgo biloba seeds are used in decoctions to help warm the body and relieve chills due to their ability to stimulate the circulation of blood and the release of body heat.

Barks: Ginkgo biloba barks are used to make decoctions that help to warm the body and relieve chills, as they contain compounds that can stimulate blood circulation and improve the body's ability to generate heat.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2-3 teaspoons of dried ginkgo biloba leaves and 1 quart of water for this herbal decoction.
Combine the dried ginkgo biloba leaves with 1 quart of water in a saucepan.
Boil the mixture for 3-5 minutes at a medium heat setting.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer for 10-15 minutes after boiling.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a cup.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint decoctions helps with chills because its menthol content provides a soothing and calming effect on the body. When consumed as a warm tea, it can help to relax the muscles and ease the sensation of coldness, providing relief from chills. Additionally, peppermint's natural warming properties can also help to stimulate blood flow and increase body temperature, further aiding in the alleviation of chill symptoms.
remedy_properties: Antipyretic property: Peppermint's antipyretic property helps to reduce fever and alleviate chills by blocking the production of prostaglandins, which can cause body temperature to rise.
Antispasmodic property: The antispasmodic properties of peppermint help to relax muscles and relieve spasms associated with chills, reducing shivering and discomfort.

Anti-inflammatory property: Peppermint's anti-inflammatory compounds may help to reduce swelling and inflammation caused by viral infections that can lead to chills, promoting a sense of comfort and relief.
remedy_constituents: Menthol: Menthol, a terpene compound found in Mentha x piperita decoctions, helps alleviate chills by stimulating the brain's cold receptors and increasing circulation to warm the body.
Menthone: Menthone, another terpene compound in the decoction, has a warming effect on the body by increasing the sensation of heat and reducing the feeling of coldness.
Rosmarinic acid: Rosmarinic acid, a phenolic compound found in Mentha x piperita, helps to reduce inflammation and promote blood flow, which can help the body warm up and combat chills.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Due to their high concentration of menthol and menthone, which have natural cooling and decongestant properties.
Roots: As they contain a higher concentration of menthol and other compounds that help to relieve chills and fever.
Buds: Since they have a higher concentration of menthol and other active compounds than mature leaves, making them more effective for decoctions targeting chills.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 grams of fresh mentha x piperita leaves in the morning.
Chop the leaves into small pieces to facilitate even infusion in boiling water.
Combine the chopped leaves with 500ml of boiling water in a heat-resistant container.
Steep the mixture for 10-15 minutes to allow the active ingredients to infuse.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender decoctions helps with chills because it possesses natural antiviral, anti-inflammatory, and calming properties. When consumed as a warm tea, the flavonoids and volatile oils in lavender work to soothe the body and mind, reducing fever and shaking sensations associated with chills. Additionally, the herb's antimicrobial properties help combat underlying infections that may be causing the chill, promoting overall well-being and relief from discomfort.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Lavender's antioxidants help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to feelings of chilliness and discomfort.
Sedative and calming effects: Lavender's sedative compounds, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, promote relaxation and calmness, helping to soothe symptoms of chills and reduce anxiety.
Vasodilatory properties: The vasodilatory compounds in lavender, such as camphor and borneol, help to dilate blood vessels, improving circulation and warm the body, reducing feelings of chilliness.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: Linalool is a terpene that helps with chills due to its analgesic and antipyretic properties, which can help reduce pain and lower body temperature.
Linalyl acetate: Linalyl acetate is a terpene ester that contributes to the antipyretic and anti-inflammatory effects of Lavandula angustifolia, helping to alleviate chills and discomfort.

Rosmarinic acid: Rosmarinic acid is a phenolic compound that possesses antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce fever and alleviate chills by mitigating the underlying infection.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used to make decoctions for chills due to their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties.
Flowers: They are used to make decoctions for chills due to their calming and antispasmodic effects, which help reduce fever and alleviate discomfort.
Stems: They are used to make decoctions for chills due to their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help soothe and calm the body.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried lavandula angustifolia flowers in the morning after dew has evaporated for better quality.
Dry the harvested flowers using a low-temperature dehydrator at 100 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Measure out 2 teaspoons of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers for every 1 cup of water.
Combine the measured flowers with boiling water in a heat-resistant glass container.
Steep the mixture for 5-10 minutes before straining and serving as a herbal decoction.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Goat's rue decoctions helps with chills because of its unique ability to warm and invigorate the body. The herb's active compounds, such as coumarin and glycosides, are known to stimulate blood circulation and increase metabolic rate, which helps to raise the body's natural temperature and alleviate feelings of chilliness. Additionally, goat's rue decoctions have been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and pneumonia, which can often cause chills in patients. By promoting a healthy inflammatory response, goat's rue decoctions may also help to reduce congestion and alleviate coughing fits.
remedy_properties: Antipyretic properties: Goat's Rue decoction has been shown to reduce fever and alleviate body heat in patients with fever-related illnesses, likely due to its ability to increase sweating and promote the breakdown of toxins.

Antimicrobial and antiviral activity: The plant contains compounds that exhibit antimicrobial and antiviral effects, which may help combat infectious agents responsible for chills and fever, such as bacteria and viruses.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Goat's Rue has been traditionally used to treat inflammatory conditions, including rheumatism, gout, and arthritis. Its anti-inflammatory properties may also contribute to its ability to relieve symptoms of chills, such as pain and stiffness.
remedy_constituents: Lithospermic acid: It helps with chills by exhibiting anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce the body's response to infection and alleviate symptoms associated with chills.
Lithospermol: It contributes to the reduction of chills by showing antimicrobial properties, which can help combat underlying infections that may be causing the chills.

Iridoid glycosides (specifically, aucubin and isoaucubin): These compounds help with chills by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help reduce the severity of chills and alleviate related symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of medicinal compounds.
Leaves: The leaves are used for their antipyretic properties, helping to reduce fever.
Barks: The barks are utilized for their warming and stimulating effects, which help to combat chills and coldness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest approximately 20-30 grams of dried roots and rhizomes from mature galega officinalis plants in late summer.
Chop the dried roots and rhizomes into small pieces to increase their surface area for infusion.
Combine 20-30 grams of chopped roots and rhizomes with 500 milliliters of boiling water in a heat-resistant container.
Steep the mixture for 5-10 minutes to allow the herbal constituents to infuse into the water.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean container to separate the liquid from solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon verbena decoctions helps with chills because of its unique composition that combines antiviral, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory properties. The decoction's citral content helps to soothe and calm the body's natural response to infection, reducing fever and shivering. Additionally, lemon verbena's warming properties stimulate blood circulation, increasing heat production and helping to raise the body's internal temperature, providing relief from chills and discomfort. As a result, lemon verbena decoctions can effectively alleviate chill symptoms, promoting overall wellness and comfort.
remedy_properties: Antipyretic property: Lemon verbena decoctions have been shown to reduce fever and alleviate chills by stimulating sweating, which helps to eliminate toxins and bring down body temperature.
Anti-inflammatory property: The decoction's anti-inflammatory compounds can help soothe and calm the body's response to infection or inflammation, reducing feelings of chilliness and discomfort.

Antiviral and antibacterial property: Lemon verbena's antiviral and antibacterial properties can help combat underlying infections that may be causing chills, such as respiratory tract infections, and prevent further spread of the infection.
remedy_constituents: Limonene: A terpene that has been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate chills by reducing inflammation in the body.
Verbascoside: A phenolic glycoside that has been found to have antipyretic properties, which can help lower fever and alleviate chills.
Asperuloside: A phenolic glycoside that has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties, which can help reduce inflammation and lower fever, thus alleviating chills.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain compounds with anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help alleviate chills and fever.
Leaves: The leaves are rich in tannins, which have a cooling effect and help reduce fever and alleviate chills.
Flowers: The flowers have antiseptic and antispasmodic properties, which aid in relieving chills and fever by promoting sweating and reducing inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried roots of verbena officinalis in the early morning or late afternoon.
Clean the roots by gently scrubbing them with a soft brush under cold running water.
Weigh out 10-15 grams of the cleaned verbena officinalis roots for a decoction.
Combine the weighed roots with 1 liter of water in a saucepan and bring to a boil.
Simmer the mixture for 5-10 minutes or until the liquid has reduced slightly and the roots are soft.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme decoctions helps with chills because of its natural antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The essential oils present in thyme, such as thymol and carvacrol, have been shown to help combat viral and bacterial infections that often cause chills. As the decoction is absorbed into the body, it helps to reduce inflammation and fever, providing relief from symptoms like shivering and sweating. Additionally, thyme's expectorant properties can also help to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, further alleviating congestion and discomfort associated with chills.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial property: Thyme's antimicrobial compounds, such as thymol and carvacrol, help to combat bacterial and viral infections that can cause chills by inhibiting their growth and reducing inflammation.

Decongestant property: The essential oils present in thyme decoctions can help to thin mucus and ease congestion in the chest and nasal passages, making it easier for the body to expel excess mucus and relieve chills.

Anti-inflammatory property: Thyme's flavonoids and phenolic acids have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help to reduce fever and alleviate symptoms of chills by decreasing inflammation and promoting healing.
remedy_constituents: Thymol: A phenolic compound found in Thymus vulgaris, thymol helps with chills due to its potent antimicrobial properties that aid in reducing fever and relieving body aches.
Carvacrol: A phenolic monoterpene, carvacrol helps with chills by acting as an antipyretic, which means it helps to lower body temperature, thus reducing the sensation of chills.

Caryophyllene oxide: A sesquiterpenoid compound, caryophyllene oxide helps with chills by exhibiting anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling and discomfort caused by chills, making it easier to manage the condition.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their high concentration of thymol, a compound with antimicrobial and antiseptic properties.
Stems: The stems of Thymus vulgaris are used for their ability to warm the body and relieve chills, with their aromatic properties stimulating circulation.
Roots: The roots are used for their ability to relieve colds and chills, with their warm and stimulating properties helping to restore body heat.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh thymus vulgaris leaves in the morning after the dew has evaporated to ensure optimal potency.
Dry the harvested leaves in a warm dry place or using a food dehydrator at 95 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Measure 1 teaspoon of dried thymus vulgaris leaves and place them in a heat-resistant glass cup.
Pour 8 ounces of boiling water over the dried leaves and let it steep for 5 to 10 minutes.
Strain the liquid using a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve and discard the solids to obtain the decoction.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosemary decoctions helps with chills because of its natural antiviral and antibacterial properties, which can help to combat underlying infections that may be causing the chill. The decongestant properties in rosemary also help to clear nasal passages and sinuses, allowing for easier breathing and a sense of relief from congestion. Additionally, rosemary's warming effects can help to stimulate circulation and raise body temperature, making it an effective natural remedy for combating chills and promoting overall wellness.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Rosemary's antimicrobial compounds, such as rosmarinic acid, help to combat invading pathogens that cause chills and fever by inhibiting their growth and reproduction.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Rosemary's anti-inflammatory compounds, including carnosol and camphor, help to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with chills, such as body aches and joint pain.

Stimulant properties: Rosemary's stimulant effects can help to warm the body from the inside out, increasing circulation and helping to raise body temperature in cases of hypothermia or chill, making it an effective treatment for chills.
remedy_constituents: Carnosic acid: Carnosic acid, a phenolic diterpene, helps reduce chills by exerting antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which may help alleviate fever-related discomfort.

Rosmarinic acid: Rosmarinic acid, a polyphenolic compound, assists in combating chills by acting as an antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory agent, which may help alleviate the underlying infection or inflammation causing the chills.
Bornyl acetate: Bornyl acetate, a monoterpenoid ester, contributes to reducing chills by exhibiting antispasmodic and analgesic properties, which may help alleviate fever-related pain and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used for their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties to help reduce fever and alleviate cold symptoms.
Stems: Used for their antiseptic properties to help combat infections and reduce inflammation associated with chills.
Flowers: Used for their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties to help reduce fever and alleviate cold symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 25g of dried rosmarinus officinalis leaves or 50g of fresh leaves for decoction.
Combine harvested rosmarinus officinalis leaves with 500ml of cold water in a heat-resistant container.
Boil the mixture over medium heat for 5-10 minutes or until the liquid has reduced slightly.
Steep the decoction for 10-15 minutes after boiling to allow flavors and oils to infuse.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm decoctions helps with chills because of its natural warming properties, which can help to increase body temperature. The herb contains compounds that stimulate blood flow and dilate blood vessels, allowing for a greater supply of warm blood to reach the extremities and core of the body. Additionally, lemon balm has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce fever and alleviate symptoms such as shivering and sweating. By promoting relaxation and calming the nervous system, lemon balm decoctions can also help to reduce feelings of cold and discomfort associated with chills.
remedy_properties: Antiviral properties: Lemon balm's antiviral compounds, such as rosmarinic acid and caffeic acid, help combat viral infections that can cause chills, reducing their severity and duration.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Lemon balm's anti-inflammatory compounds, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, soothe and calm the body's response to infection, reducing inflammation and relieving symptoms such as fever and chills.
Antipyretic properties: Lemon balm's antipyretic compounds, including citral and geraniol, help regulate body temperature, reducing fever and chills by promoting sweating and heat dissipation.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinicine (a phenolic compound): This compound helps to reduce fever and alleviate chills due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
Melissic acid (a terpene): This compound contributes to the antimicrobial properties of Melissa officinalis, helping to combat underlying infections that may be causing chills.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 grams of melissa officinalis leaves and flowers from a clean and dry environment.
Chop the harvested melissa officinalis into small pieces to increase surface area.
Combine the chopped melissa officinalis with 500 milliliters of boiling water in a glass container.
Steep the mixture for 10-15 minutes to allow the melissa officinalis to release its properties.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elder decoctions helps with chills because they contain compounds that have a warming effect on the body. The saponins and flavonoids in elderflowers, for example, increase blood flow to the skin's surface, helping to raise body temperature and reduce shivering. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory properties of elderberry help to alleviate cold-related discomforts such as headaches and fatigue. As a result, drinking an elder decoction can provide quick relief from chills and help to restore warmth and comfort to the body.
remedy_properties: Antiviral and antibacterial properties: Elder decoction's antiviral and antibacterial compounds help combat viral and bacterial infections that can cause chills, reducing their severity.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in elder decoction reduce inflammation and swelling in the body, which can contribute to feelings of chilliness and discomfort.

Expectorant properties: Elder decoction's expectorant compounds help loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, making it easier to breathe and reducing congestion, which can be a contributing factor to chills.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These plant compounds help alleviate chills by modulating the immune system and exhibiting anti-inflammatory properties, which contribute to relieving symptoms associated with chills and fever.
Sambunigrin (a type of saponin): Sambunigrin acts as an anti-inflammatory agent and helps to reduce the severity of chills by soothing the body and alleviating discomfort associated with fever.

Phenolic acids (such as ferulic acid): These compounds possess antioxidant properties, which help combat oxidative stress and contribute to reducing the body's temperature, thus alleviating chills and fever.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers of Sambucus nigra are used to make decoctions for chills because they contain antiviral and antibacterial properties that help alleviate symptoms.
Stems: The stems of Sambucus nigra are used to make decoctions for chills because they have antipyretic properties that help reduce fever.
Leaves: The leaves of Sambucus nigra are used to make decoctions for chills because they contain flavonoids and other compounds that help reduce inflammation and alleviate cold symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Gather 60-120 grams of sambucus nigra branches and leaves for a standard decoction.
Crush the plant material into smaller pieces using a mortar and pestle.
Combine the crushed plant material with 1 liter of water in a saucepan.
Bring the mixture to a boil then reduce the heat and simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion decoctions helps with chills because of its natural antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties. The diuretic and expectorant compounds in dandelion root and leaves help to flush out toxins and congestion from the body, promoting a sense of warmth and comfort. Additionally, the antioxidant-rich properties of dandelion soothe the nervous system, reducing symptoms of chilliness such as shivering and cold sweats. By increasing circulation and warming the body from within, dandelion decoctions provide natural relief for chills and associated discomforts.
remedy_properties: Inflammation reduction: Dandelion decoctions contain compounds like taraxasterol, which can reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of fever and chills.
Antibacterial properties: The decoction's high concentration of flavonoids and terpenes provides antibacterial properties that help combat bacterial infections contributing to chills.
Diuretic effects: Dandelion decoctions stimulate urine production, which can help rid the body of excess heat, toxins, and irritants, thereby reducing the severity of chills.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of dandelion are rich in inulin, a natural prebiotic that can help stimulate digestion and warm the body from within, making it effective against chills.
Buds: Dandelion buds are packed with antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and calm the body's response to feverish chills.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried taraxacum officinale leaves and flowers in amounts of 20-30 grams for every 500 milliliters water.
Rinse the herb material with cold running water to remove any dirt or debris and gently pat it dry with paper towels.
Combine the rinsed herb material with 500 milliliters boiling water in a heat-resistant glass container and let steep for 15-20 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh strainer into another container to remove any solid particles and discard the solids.
Allow the decoction to cool to room temperature and store it in an amber glass bottle refrigerated at 2-8 degrees celsius for up to 24 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chinese peony decoctions helps with chills because of its unique properties that warm and dispel cold. The herbs used in the decoction, such as Shao Yao (peony root) and Chuan Lian Bai Cao (white mulberry leaf), have been traditionally used to treat fever and cold-related symptoms. When taken as a hot tea, the decoction helps to raise the body's internal temperature, thereby dispelling chills and promoting sweating. This natural warming effect also helps to relieve body aches and discomfort associated with chills, providing relief from unpleasant symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Peony's anti-inflammatory effects help to reduce swelling and pain associated with chills, making it an effective treatment for conditions like arthritis and rheumatism.
Warming property: The warming effect of Peony helps to dispel coldness and relieve chills by stimulating blood circulation and warming the body from within.

Antiviral property: Peony's antiviral properties help to combat viral infections that can cause chills, such as common colds and flu, by inhibiting the replication of viruses and reducing the severity of symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the Chinese Peony plant have been traditionally used to treat fevers and chills due to their antipyretic properties, which help to reduce body temperature.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes (underground stems) of the Chinese Peony plant are used to make decoctions that can help to alleviate symptoms of chills and fever, such as sweating and shivering.
remedy_recipe: Gather high-quality dried paeonia lactiflora roots and prepare the necessary equipment.
Measure out 15-30 grams of the dried root and place it in a heat-resistant container.
Combine the root with 6-8 cups of water in a pot and bring to a boil.
Reduce heat to low and simmer for 1.5-2 hours or overnight to extract the active compounds.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean container and discard solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile decoctions helps with chills because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe the body's cooling response. The apigenin content in chamomile binds to GABA receptors in the brain, promoting relaxation and calming the nervous system. This reduces stress and anxiety, allowing the body's internal thermostat to regulate its temperature more effectively, alleviating chills and feverish sensations. Additionally, chamomile's anti-inflammatory properties also help to ease muscle cramps and spasms that often accompany chills, providing a sense of comfort and relief.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic property: Chamomile's antispasmodic effects help to relax muscles and reduce muscle spasms, which can contribute to chills by reducing inflammation and alleviating discomfort.

Anti-inflammatory property: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as apigenin and luteolin, help to reduce swelling and ease pain associated with chills, promoting a sense of warmth and comfort.

Sedative property: Chamomile's sedative effects help to calm the nervous system, reducing anxiety and stress that can exacerbate chills, promoting relaxation and helping the body to regulate its natural temperature.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of chamomile are used because they contain a high concentration of apigenin, an antioxidant that helps to reduce inflammation and fever.
Flowers: The flowers of chamomile are used to make soothing and calming teas that help to ease symptoms of chills, such as restlessness and anxiety.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried matricaria chamomilla flowers in sufficient quantities for your desired dosage.
Measure out one teaspoon of dried chamomile flowers or three teaspoons of fresh chamomile flowers.
Combine the measured ingredients with 8 ounces of boiling water in a heat-resistant glass container.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes, allowing the herbs to infuse and release their therapeutic properties.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a clean cup, discarding solids and enjoying the warm tea.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel decoctions helps with chills because it has a natural warming effect on the body. The herb contains compounds that stimulate digestion and relieve spasms in the gastrointestinal tract, which can help to alleviate feelings of coldness and discomfort. Additionally, fennel's expectorant properties can help to clear congestion and ease coughs, further reducing the sensation of chilliness. By promoting circulation and warming the body from within, fennel decoctions provide a natural and soothing solution for combating chills and flu-like symptoms.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic property: Fennel's antispasmodic property helps to relax muscles and relieve muscle cramps, which can be exacerbated by chills.
Warming and anti-inflammatory properties: Fennel's warming and anti-inflammatory properties help to increase blood flow, reduce swelling, and alleviate the discomfort caused by chills.
Expectorant property: Fennel's expectorant property helps to loosen and clear mucus from the respiratory tract, making it easier to breathe and relieving congestion associated with chills.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in volatile oils and saponins, which help to reduce fever and ease congestion.
Seeds: Contain a compound called anethole, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to relieve cold and flu symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried foeniculum vulgare leaves and flowers in quantities of one ounce.
Combine the harvested material with four cups of boiling water and steep for 10 minutes.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a large bowl to remove solids.
Allow the decoction to cool to room temperature then refrigerate for at least two hours before use.
Store the chilled decoction in an airtight container and consume within 24 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger decoctions helps with chills because it increases blood circulation, which helps to warm up the body from the inside out. The warming properties of ginger also help to counteract the cooling effect of sweat, providing relief from shivering and discomfort. Additionally, ginger has natural anti-inflammatory compounds that can help to reduce fever and alleviate symptoms associated with colds and flu. By promoting relaxation and reducing stress, ginger decoctions can also help to soothe the body's response to chills.
remedy_properties: Vasodilatory property: Ginger decoctions can dilate blood vessels, which helps to increase blood flow and warm the body, relieving coldness and shivering.
Anti-inflammatory property: Ginger's anti-inflammatory compounds may help reduce swelling and alleviate discomfort associated with chills and fever, promoting a sense of warmth and comfort.

Expectorant property: The volatile oils present in ginger decoctions can help loosen and clear mucus from the respiratory tract, which can help relieve congestion and sinus pressure often accompanying chills.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rich in bioactive compounds, rhyzomes help to relieve chills and reduce fever by promoting sweating and relieving congestion.
Roots: Containing anti-inflammatory properties, roots of the ginger plant aid in reducing chill symptoms such as body aches and pains.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2-3 inches of fresh ginger rhizome cut into small pieces.
Combine the ginger pieces in a clean glass jar or container.
Add 8 ounces of boiling water over the ginger and let it steep.
Allow the mixture to infuse for 10-15 minutes then strain and discard solids.
Transfer the decoction to a cup and drink immediately while still warm.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Joe pye weed decoctions helps with chills because of its natural antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory properties. The decoction's bioactive compounds, including flavonoids and sesquiterpenes, work to relax muscles and reduce fever-induced spasms. This helps to alleviate the shivering sensation associated with chills, promoting a sense of warmth and comfort. Additionally, joe pye weed's antipyretic properties help to lower body temperature, further alleviating chill symptoms. By addressing the root causes of chills, joe pye weed decoctions offer a natural and effective remedy for relief from these uncomfortable symptoms.
remedy_properties: Antiviral properties: Joe Pye Weed's antiviral properties help combat viral infections, such as the common cold and flu, which can cause chills.

Expectorant properties: The expectorant properties of Joe Pye Weed decoctions help loosen and clear mucus from the respiratory tract, relieving congestion and chill-related discomforts like coughing and sore throat.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Joe Pye Weed's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce swelling and inflammation in the body, which can contribute to chills and fever by reducing blood flow and heat distribution.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, the roots are used to treat fever and chills due to their antipyretic properties.
Barks: The barks contain tannins, which help to reduce inflammation and relieve symptoms associated with chills.
remedy_recipe: Gather one ounce of dried eupatorium perfoliatum herb for every cup of water needed.
Combine the herb and water in a saucepan and bring the mixture to a boil.
Reduce heat and simmer the concoction for 15-20 minutes allowing the active compounds release.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean container discarding solids.
Allow the decoction to cool before refrigerating it for up to 24 hours for later use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow decoctions helps with chills because it is a natural antipyretic, capable of reducing fever and alleviating shivering. The plant's active compounds, such as coumarins and phenolic acids, have anti-inflammatory properties that help to calm the body's response to infection or illness, thereby lowering body temperature. Additionally, yarrow's expectorant properties can help to relieve congestion and sinus pressure associated with chills, promoting overall respiratory health and well-being. As a result, drinking yarrow decoctions can provide relief from unpleasant symptoms and promote a speedy recovery.
remedy_properties: Antipyretic properties: Yarrow's decoction helps to reduce fever by inhibiting the release of prostaglandins, which are responsible for causing inflammation and increasing body temperature.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Yarrow's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, help to reduce swelling and alleviate symptoms associated with chills, such as pain and stiffness.

Antimicrobial properties: Yarrow's antimicrobial activity helps to combat infections that can cause chills, including bacterial and viral infections, by inhibiting the growth of pathogenic microorganisms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used due to their ability to promote sweating, which helps to relieve chills and fever.
Rhizomes: The rhizomes have antipyretic properties, making them effective in reducing body temperature and alleviating chill symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh achillea millefolium leaves and flowers in the morning for maximum potency.
Rinse the harvested plant material with clean water to remove any dirt or debris.
Combine one tablespoon of dried achillea millefolium herb or three teaspoons of fresh herb with two cups of boiling water.
Steep the mixture for 15-20 minutes in a ceramic or glass container to allow for maximum extraction.
Strain and discard solids before drinking the decoction hot, ideally within one hour of preparation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Milk thistle decoctions helps with chills because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which aid in reducing body temperature. The silymarin compounds present in milk thistle work to alleviate inflammation in the body, thereby helping to calm down fever and shivering. Additionally, milk thistle's antioxidant properties help to soothe and warm the body from within, providing relief from chills and discomfort associated with colds and flu. As a natural remedy, milk thistle decoctions offer a safe and effective way to combat chills and promote overall well-being.
remedy_properties: Silymarin: Helps to reduce fever and alleviate chills by exhibiting antipyretic (fever-reducing) and anti-inflammatory effects, which can soothe the body's response to infection or illness.

Flavonoids: Act as antioxidants to neutralize free radicals and protect the body from oxidative stress, which can help combat the underlying causes of chills and fever, such as inflammation and tissue damage.

Saponins: Possess antimicrobial properties that can help inhibit the growth of pathogens, reducing the severity and duration of illness-related symptoms like chills and fever, by preventing bacterial and viral replication.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of milk thistle are used to make decoctions that help to warm the body and relieve cold-induced shivering.
Seeds: Milk thistle seeds contain silymarin, a compound that helps to reduce inflammation and fever associated with chills.
remedy_recipe: Gather 10-15 grams of dried silybum marianum flowers and stems for this medicinal preparation.
Combine the herb with 500ml of boiling water in a large glass container.
Steep the mixture for 30 minutes, allowing the herb to release its active compounds into the liquid.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass bottle.
Store the prepared decoction in the refrigerator for up to 3 days or freeze for later use.

supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to chills that are treated with herbal decoctions are fever, rheumatism, arthritis, and flu. Decoctions made from herbs like ginger, cinnamon, peppermint, and eucalyptus help alleviate symptoms such as body aches, joint pain, and congestion by reducing inflammation and promoting sweating. These herbal remedies can also help to restore the body's natural temperature and ease discomfort associated with these conditions.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal decoctions that help with chills is a blend of ginger, cinnamon, and peppermint. Ginger's warming properties and anti-inflammatory compounds help to soothe the body and reduce fever. Cinnamon's antimicrobial properties fight off underlying infections, while peppermint's cooling and decongestant properties help to relieve congestion and promote sweating, which is essential for flushing out toxins and breaking the fever cycle. This combination can be made into a tea or decoction to provide quick relief from chills.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale decoctions helps with chills because they possess natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which aid in soothing the body and reducing temperature fluctuations. The warming effects of ginger, a component of Zingiber officinale, stimulate circulation and help to dispel coldness. Additionally, ginger's antimicrobial properties can help to combat underlying infections that may be contributing to the chill. Regular consumption of ginger decoctions may also boost the immune system, further helping the body to recover from illness.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: These compounds have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which help alleviate body chills by reducing inflammation and relieving pain.
Shogaols: These compounds have antipyretic properties, which help lower body temperature and alleviate chills by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory substances.

Gingerols' active metabolite, [6]-Gingerol's breakdown product, 6-Gingerdiol: This compound has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help alleviate chills by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are the most commonly used part of ginger for decoctions as they contain the highest concentration of active compounds.
Buds: Ginger buds are used for decoctions due to their high concentration of volatile oils, which help to reduce fever and relieve chills.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1-2 teaspoons of dried zingiber officinale rhizome roots and store them in a clean container.
Boil 2 cups of water in a medium saucepan for 5-7 minutes or until it reaches a rolling boil.
Add the dried zingiber officinale rhizome roots to the boiling water and let them steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a clean container and discard the solids.
Allow the decoction to cool to room temperature before storing it in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea decoctions helps with chills because it stimulates the immune system, enhancing the body's natural defense against infection. The active compounds, including alkylamides and polyphenols, have anti-inflammatory properties that reduce the severity of cold and flu symptoms. The decoction also exhibits antiviral activity, inhibiting the replication of viruses that cause chills and fever. This natural remedy can help alleviate the discomfort associated with chills, promoting a faster recovery and overall well-being. Its effectiveness has been attributed to its ability to boost the immune system.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid glycosides: These compounds help reduce the severity of chills by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines, which are involved in the body's inflammatory response to infection or illness.

Alkylamides: Alkylamides in Echinacea purpurea have anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects, which help mitigate the body's response to infection and alleviate symptoms of chills, such as fever and shivering.

Caffeic acid derivatives: These compounds exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help protect the body from oxidative stress and reduce the severity of chills by promoting a healthy immune response and reducing inflammation.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used for their rich content of echinacoside and alkylamides, which are believed to have immune-boosting properties.
Flowers: The flowers are used for their high concentration of caffeic acid and other phenolic compounds, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects.
Leaves: The leaves are used for their content of phenolic acids and glycosides, which may help to reduce fever and alleviate symptoms of chills.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 cups of fresh echinacea purpurea flowers and roots in late summer or early fall season.
Dry the harvested plant material in a warm place away from direct sunlight for 1-2 weeks.
Grind 1-2 teaspoons of dried echinacea purpurea flowers and roots into a fine powder using a mortar.
Combine 1 cup of boiling water with 1-2 teaspoons of the ground plant material in a heat-resistant cup.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes then strain and discard the solids to create a decoction.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra decoctions helps with chills because of its unique combination of active compounds, primarily glycyrrhizin. This substance has potent anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce fever and alleviate chills. Additionally, glycyrrhizin has been shown to have a positive effect on the body's thermoregulation, helping to stabilize body temperature. Furthermore, the decoction's expectorant properties aid in relieving congestion, allowing the body to more effectively rid itself of pathogens and toxins that can cause chills. This makes it a popular remedy for those experiencing colds and flu.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: Saponins help with chills by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties, which can reduce body temperature and alleviate related symptoms.
Flavonoids: Flavonoids aid in combating chills by possessing antioxidant properties that help stabilize the body's thermoregulatory mechanisms and mitigate oxidative stress associated with fever.

Glycyrrhetinic acid: Glycyrrhetinic acid contributes to alleviating chills by exerting anti-inflammatory effects and inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines, which can help lower body temperature.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are the primary source of glycyrrhizin, a compound with anti-inflammatory and anti-viral properties that helps alleviate chills and fever.
Barks: The barks of Glycyrrhiza glabra contain various compounds that help reduce fever and provide relief from chills.
Leaves: The leaves of the plant are also used to make decoctions that help alleviate chills, fever, and other symptoms associated with respiratory infections.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of dried roots of glycyrrhiza glabra and cut them into small pieces.
Combine the root pieces with 8 parts of water in a saucepan and bring to boil.
Reduce the heat to low and let the mixture simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into a clean container and discard the solids.
Allow the decoction to cool down and store it in the refrigerator for up to 3 days.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba decoctions helps with chills because of its unique properties that promote warmth and circulation. The leaves of the Ginkgo biloba tree contain bioactive compounds that enhance blood flow to extremities, thus reducing the sensation of coldness. Additionally, Ginkgo biloba's natural vasodilatory effects help to dilate blood vessels, allowing warmth to spread throughout the body more efficiently. This results in an overall feeling of relief from chills, making Ginkgo biloba decoctions a popular remedy for cold and flu symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: Help with chills by exerting antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can alleviate symptoms of cold and fever.
Bilobalide: Contributes to the reduction of chills by acting as a neuroprotectant and vasodilator, improving blood flow and reducing inflammation.
Quercetin: Helps alleviate chills by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can reduce the severity of symptoms associated with cold and fever.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves of Ginkgo biloba are commonly used in decoctions for chills because they contain flavonoids and terpenoids with anti-inflammatory properties.
Buds: Buds of Ginkgo biloba are used in decoctions for chills due to their potential to reduce fever and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.
Leaves: The leaves are also used for their antipyretic properties, helping to reduce fever and alleviate symptoms of chills.
remedy_recipe: Harvest ginkgo biloba leaves in the fall when the leaves are fully ripe and dry.
Clean the leaves thoroughly by rinsing them with water to remove any dirt or debris.
Chop the ginkgo biloba leaves into small pieces to increase their surface area for decoction.
Combine 1-2 teaspoons of the chopped leaves with 1 quart of boiling water for a decoction.
Steep the mixture for 3-5 minutes and then strain the liquid to obtain the decoction.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinchona officinalis decoctions helps with chills because they contain quinine, a natural compound with antipyretic and anti-inflammatory properties. Quinine works by inhibiting the production of prostaglandins, which are hormone-like substances that cause fever and inflammation. As a result, quinine helps to lower body temperature and relieve chills. Additionally, Cinchona officinalis has been traditionally used to treat malaria, a disease that often causes chills. Its decoctions are also used to treat other fevers and colds, making it a popular remedy for relieving chills and promoting overall well-being.
remedy_constituents: Quinine: Acts as an antipyretic agent to reduce fever and alleviate chills by inhibiting the production of prostaglandins, which are responsible for inducing a fever response in the body.
Cinchonine: Exhibits antimalarial and antipyretic properties, helping to combat chills and fever by reducing the activity of the hypothalamus, the body's temperature regulation center.
Quinidine: Functions as an antimalarial and antipyretic agent, helping to alleviate chills and fever by modulating the immune response and reducing the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are rich in quinine, a key alkaloid responsible for the plant's medicinal properties.
Barks: The barks contain alkaloids that help reduce fever and alleviate chills.
Stems: The stems also contain quinine and other alkaloids that aid in treating chills and fever.
remedy_recipe: Gather 20-30 grams of dried cinchona officinalis bark from a reputable source and store it in an airtight container.
Crush the cinchona officinalis bark into small pieces using a mortar and pestle or a coffee grinder.
Combine 1-2 teaspoons of crushed cinchona officinalis with 1 liter of boiling water in a heat-resistant container.
Allow the mixture to steep for 10-15 minutes and then strain it through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve.
Discard the solids and store the decoction in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours before consumption.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus decoctions helps with chills because they contain potent compounds like eucalyptol, which has natural anti-inflammatory and decongestant properties. When consumed as a warm decoction, these compounds work to break down mucus and ease congestion, thereby reducing the feeling of chilliness. Additionally, eucalyptus has a cooling effect on the body, helping to lower fever and provide relief from shivering and discomfort associated with chills. This makes eucalyptus globulus decoctions a popular natural remedy for colds and flu.
remedy_constituents: Eucalyptol: This terpene acts as a natural decongestant, helping to relieve chills and body aches by opening up airways and increasing blood flow.
Catechin: A type of phenolic compound, catechin has anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties, which help reduce fever and alleviate chills.

Flavonoids (including Quercetin): These plant-derived compounds have potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help combat the underlying causes of chills and promote overall recovery.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Eucalyptus globulus are the most commonly used part for decoctions to treat chills due to their high concentration of bioactive compounds such as eucalyptol and flavonoids.
Barks: The barks are used for decoctions to treat chills as they contain a high amount of tannins and flavonoids that have anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties.
Stems: The stems of Eucalyptus globulus are also used for decoctions to treat chills as they contain a mixture of bioactive compounds that help to reduce fever and relieve symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Gather 4-6 fresh or dried eucalyptus globulus leaves weighing about 10-15 grams for this herbal decoction recipe.
Cut the leaves into smaller pieces to increase the surface area for better extraction of the active compounds.
Combine the cut eucalyptus leaves with 250 milliliters of boiling water in a heat-resistant glass or ceramic container.
Allow the mixture to steep for 10-15 minutes to enable the release of the herb's medicinal properties into the water.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean container and discard the solids.

remedies.remedy_desc: Astragalus membranaceus decoctions helps with chills because they stimulate the immune system and increase its ability to fight off invading pathogens. The plant's active compounds, such as astragalosides, have anti-inflammatory properties that reduce fever and alleviate symptoms associated with chills. Additionally, Astragalus membranaceus is believed to have adaptogenic properties, allowing the body to adapt to stress and promoting overall well-being. This natural remedy has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for centuries to boost the immune system and combat illness.
remedy_constituents: Isorhapontigenin: Helps reduce inflammation and fever associated with chills.
Astragaloside IV: Exhibits anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects, which can help alleviate chills.
Quercetin: Acts as an antioxidant and anti-inflammatory agent, which can help reduce the severity of chills.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used due to their high content of saponins and polysaccharides, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory and immune-boosting properties.
Stems: The stems are used for their saponin and flavonoid content, which are thought to have fever-reducing and anti-inflammatory effects.
Leaves: The leaves are used for their saponin and alkaloid content, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory and fever-reducing properties.
remedy_recipe: Gather 9-18 grams of dried astragalus membranaceus root and 3-6 grams of dried licorice root.
Combine the herbs in a saucepan and add 2-4 cups of water for a decoction.
Heat the mixture over low-medium heat for 20-30 minutes or until the liquid reduces slightly.
Strain the decoction into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove solids.
Allow the decoction to cool before serving and drinking the entire amount in one day.

remedies.remedy_desc: Andrographis paniculata decoctions helps with chills because they contain andrographolide, a compound with potent anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The decoctions work by reducing the body's inflammatory response and fighting off underlying infections that may be causing the chills. By modulating the immune system and reducing fever, Andrographis paniculata decoctions provide relief from chills and help restore balance to the body. This natural remedy has been used in traditional medicine for centuries to combat various health conditions, including those associated with chills and fever.
remedy_constituents: Neohesperidin: Acts as a flavonoid that helps reduce fever and alleviate chills due to its anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties.
Andrographolide: Exhibits anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects, which helps in reducing the body's response to pathogens and alleviating chills.

Hesperidin: Functions as a flavanone glycoside that helps reduce fever and alleviate chills due to its anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties, as well as its ability to improve blood circulation.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are the most commonly used part of Andrographis paniculata due to their high concentration of andrographolide, a key bioactive compound.
Leaves: The leaves are used to make decoctions for chills as they contain a significant amount of andrographolide, which helps to reduce fever and alleviate symptoms.
Stems: The stems, particularly the upper parts, are also utilized for decoctions as they contain a substantial amount of andrographolide, which contributes to their medicinal properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh andrographis paniculata leaves and stems from well-maintained gardens or cultivated areas by hand.
Clean the harvested material thoroughly with water to remove dirt and debris from the surface.
Dry the cleaned andrographis paniculata leaves and stems in a well-ventilated area for 3 to 5 days.
Combine 2 to 3 grams of dried andrographis paniculata leaves and stems with 200 milliliters of boiling water in a pot.
Steep the mixture for 5 to 10 minutes and then strain it to obtain the decoction.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ephedra sinica decoctions helps with chills because of its inherent properties that stimulate the body's natural response to fight off cold temperatures. The decoction's active compounds, such as ephedrine and pseudoephedrine, increase blood circulation and metabolism, which in turn raises the body's internal temperature. Additionally, Ephedra sinica contains anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that help combat underlying infections, thus alleviating the symptoms of chills and promoting overall well-being. This natural remedy has been used for centuries to combat colds and flu.
remedy_constituents: Alkaloids: They help with chills by stimulating the nervous system and increasing body temperature through their vasoconstrictive and thermogenic properties.
Phenylethanoids: They help with chills by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that reduce discomfort and fever associated with chills.
Terpenoids: They help with chills by showing antiviral and immunomodulatory effects, which can aid in fighting off underlying infections that cause chills.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Ephedra sinica are used to make decoctions due to their high ephedrine content, which helps to warm the body and relieve chills.
Seeds: The seeds of Ephedra sinica are used for decoctions as they are believed to have a warming and decongestant effect, helping to dispel cold and relieve chills.
Stems: The stems of Ephedra sinica are used for decoctions as they contain a significant amount of ephedrine and other alkaloids, which help to warm the body and relieve chills.
remedy_recipe: Harvest ephedra sinica roots and stems in late summer or early autumn when the plant is mature.
Clean and dry the harvested ephedra sinica roots and stems thoroughly for 2 days.
Crush or grind the dried ephedra sinica roots and stems into fine powder using a mill.
Combine 9 grams of ephedra sinica powder with 3 cups of water in a saucepan.
Simmer the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes to 1 hour then strain and discard.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lonicera japonica decoctions helps with chills because it has anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties. The plant contains compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, which can help to reduce fever and alleviate chills by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines. Additionally, the decoction's expectorant properties can help to relieve respiratory congestion, a common cause of chills, and promote sweat production to help the body regulate its temperature. This natural remedy can provide relief from chills and other symptoms associated with fever.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These compounds help with chills by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which aid in reducing fever and alleviating symptoms associated with colds and flu.
Phenolic acids: These compounds assist in combating chills by possessing anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties, which help to lower body temperature and reduce the severity of fever.
Glycosides: These compounds aid in relieving chills by demonstrating anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which help to soothe the body and alleviate symptoms associated with colds and flu.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are used due to their warming and anti-inflammatory properties.
Leaves: They are used because of their ability to dispel cold and relieve fever.
Stems: They are used for their warming and dehumidifying effects, which help to treat chills.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the lonicera japonica flowers and leaves in the morning after the dew has dried for freshness.
Dry the lonicera japonica flowers and leaves in a warm and well-ventilated area for 2-3 days.
Measure out 3-6 grams of dried lonicera japonica flowers and leaves for each 100ml of water.
Steep the dried lonicera japonica flowers and leaves in boiling water for 5-10 minutes to release the active compounds.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean container for consumption.
